content.full_text:
May 11, 1990

Mr. John Wallace  
Counsel, Legal Division  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This is a request for an advice letter pertaining to a matter discussed recently with you by telephone.

As a Governor's appointee, I am required to file an annual Statement of Economic Interests, form 730.

The Independent Warranty Servicers (IWS), a division of the California State Electronic Association (CSEA) is planning a dinner for November 10, 1990 at the Compri Hotel in San Pedro, California, honoring me as "Man of the Year".

CSEA is an independent association of service dealers that are registrants of the Bureau of Electronic and Appliance Repair (BEAR). The BEAR registers and regulates electronic and appliance service dealers in California.

The IWS is a recently formed division of CSEA and plans to honor a different person every year from this year forward.

With regard to gifts, I understand IWS will present a journal of testimonials to me regarding my 25 years of service with the BEAR. Space for testimonials in the journal will be offered to electronic and appliance manufacturers and service dealers that are familiar with my career.

I was told that I would be provided a complimentary room at the hotel (no cost to me or IWS) since IWS is renting the banquet room in the hotel for the dinner.

IWS is charging those who wish to attend, $75 for the dinner. I assume my dinner will be complimentary.

I believe the above includes all the information regarding "gifts" at the dinner in my honor.

Your assistance in providing an advice letter in the matter my reporting obligations, will certainly be appreciated. Please call me at 445-4751 if further information is required.

Sincerely,

[Signature]

WILLIAM J. HAYES
c/o Bureau of Electronic and Appliance Repair
909 S Street
Sacramento, CA 95814
